page: Vultr vcg-a16-12c-128g-32vram is a GPU-accelerated virtual server configured with 12 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 128.0 GB of system memory, and a 700 GB local NVMe SSD. The graphics acceleration is delivered by two NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs providing a combined 32 GB of VRAM. This hardware configuration offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 10.67 GB per core alongside dedicated hardware acceleration, making it a resource-dense option for workloads requiring parallel processing and substantial memory capacity.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in machine learning and data processing. In LLM inference tests using a medium 7B model, the server achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds of 613.27 tokens per second and a strong text generation rate of 32.35 tokens per second. For smaller 135M models, prompt processing reaches 8665.1 tokens per second. Standard compute benchmarks show average multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 21080.54 and a Passmark CPU score of 20085.48, reflecting the shared nature of the Broadwell-based vCPUs.

The server balances high-density GPU and memory resources with shared CPU allocation, presenting a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive and graphics-accelerated tasks that do not require dedicated, high-frequency CPU cores. It is well-suited for medium-scale LLM inference, database operations, and parallel data processing pipelines that leverage GPU acceleration and local NVMe storage.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-12c-128g-32vram is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 12 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and a 700 GB NVMe SSD. It is equipped with two NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs providing a total of 32 GB of VRAM. Benchmarks highlight top-tier performance in LLM inference, with prompt processing speeds reaching 613.27 tokens per second on medium 7B models. While CPU performance is average due to the shared Broadwell architecture, the high memory-to-core ratio of 10.67 GB and dual GPUs deliver excellent resource density. This server is optimized for GPU-accelerated workloads, medium-scale LLM inference, and memory-intensive database operations.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-12c-128g-32vram is a GPU-accelerated cloud server configured with 12 shared Intel vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, a 700 GB NVMe SSD, and dual NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs providing 32 GB of total VRAM.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-12c-128g-32vram is a cloud server featuring 12 shared Intel vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, a 700 GB NVMe SSD, and two NVIDIA A16 GPUs with 32 GB VRAM.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual server featuring dual NVIDIA Ampere GPUs, high memory density, and local NVMe storage for parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs with 32 GB total VRAM
12 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs and 128 GB of system memory
Top-tier LLM inference prompt processing speeds
Suited for medium-scale machine learning and database operations
